name: Foolproof Sourdough Starter 3
recipeInstructions:
1. In a pottery or glass bowl, dissolve the yeast in half a cup of warm water.
2. Stir in the remaining warm water and flour until well combined.
3. Transfer the mixture to a plastic, glass, or ceramic container and cover it.
4. Allow the mixture to stand at room temperature (near but not directly over the stove pilot light) for 3-5 days.
5. When ready to use, refrigerate the starter. As the liquid rises to the top, gently mix with a fork before using.
6. Feed the starter every 5 days by adding half a cup of flour and one-third cup of warm water.
7. Each time you use the starter, reserve at least one cup, replacing the amount taken out with equal parts flour and water.
